author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice
book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

metadata.author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice

title: Canon IV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: That Bishops are to abstain from all receiving of gifts.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: The Church’s herald, Paul the divine Apostle, laying down a rule (κανόνα) not only for the presbyters of Ephesus but for the whole company of the priesthood, speaks thus explicitly, saying, “I have coveted no man’s silver or gold, or apparel.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: I have shewed you all things, how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak;” for he accounted it more blessed to give.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Therefore we being taught by him do decree, that under no circumstances, shall a Bishop for the sake of filthy lucre invent feigned excuses for sins, and exact gold or silver or other gifts from the bishops, clergy, or monks who are subject to him.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: For says the Apostle, “The unrighteous shall not possess the kingdom of God,” and, “The children ought not to lay up for the parents, but the parents for the children.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: If then any is found, who for the sake of exacting gold or any other gift, or who from personal feeling, has suspended from the ministry, or even excommunicated, any of the clergy subject to his jurisdiction, or who has closed any of the venerable temples, so that the service of God may not be celebrated in it, pouring out his madness even upon things insensible, and thus shewing himself to be without understanding, he shall be subjected to the same punishment he devised for others, and his trouble shall return on his own head, as a transgressor of God’s commandment and of the apostolic precepts.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: For Peter the supreme head (ἡ κερυφαία ἀκρότης) of the Apostles commands, “Feed the flock of God which is among you, taking the oversight thereof, not by constraint, but willingly; not for filthy lucre but of a ready mind; neither as being lords over the clergy (τῶν κλήρων [A.V.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: God’s heritage]); but being ensamples to the flock.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: And when the chief shepherd shall appear, ye shall receive a crown of glory that fadeth not away.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon IV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: We decree that no bishop shall extort gold or silver, or anything else from bishops, clerics, or monks subject to his jurisdiction.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: And if anyone through the power of gold or of any other thing or through his own whims, shall be found to have prevented any one of the clergy who are subject to him, from the celebration of the holy offices, or shall have shut up a venerable temple so that the sacred worship of God could not be performed in it, he shall be subject to the lex talionis.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: For Peter the Apostle says: Feed the flock of God, not of necessity but willingly, and according to God; not for filthy lucre’s sake, but with a prompt mind; not exercising lordship over the clergy, but being an example to the flock.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Note the present canon, which punishes those bishops by the lex talionis, who for filthy lucre’s sake, or out of private affection, separate any from themselves, or close temples.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Wherefore he who cuts off others thus, let him be cut off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: But he who shuts off a temple shall be punished even more than by cutting off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: But lest any one should say, by the argument á contrario, that a bishop should not be punished who neither for the sake of filthy lucre nor out of private spite, but lawfully cuts some off, or closes temples, I answer that this argument only holds good of the cutting off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: For a bishop who for any reason, whether just or unjust, shuts up a temple, should be punished, so it seems to me, as I have said above.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It would seem that at that time among the Greeks the use of local interdict (interdicti localis) was not known.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: But very many theologians wish to find a vestige of this interdict in the IVth century, in St. Basil’s epistle cclxx. (otherwise ccxliv.), where the holy doctor teaches that the person who carries off by force a virgin, and those who are cognizant of this wickedness ought to be smitten with excommunication, and that the village or its inhabitants, to which the ravisher shall escape and where he shall be kept in safety, shall be shut out from the prayers.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: This canon, or rather the first part of it, is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars II., Causa XVI., Q.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: I., Canon lxiv.; all the latter part is represented by the words “et infra.”
